question_text: O 3 2├бРѓгРђю , reacts in acidic conditions as shown. S O 3 2├бРѓгРђю (aq) + 2H + (aq) S(s) + SO O(l) You will investigate how the concentration of the thiosulfate ions affects the rate of this reaction. The rate can be measured by timing how long it takes for the solid sulfur that is formed to make the solution too cloudy to see through. Small amounts of SO 2 gas may be produced during this reaction. Care must be taken to avoid inhaling this SO 2 gas. It is very important that as soon as each experiment is complete the beaker containing the reaction mixture is emptied into the quenching bath. FA 1 is 0.100 mol dm ├бРѓгРђю3 sodium thiosulfate, Na O 3 . question_text: A student plans to carry out an experiment to find the relative molecular mass, M r , of a soluble acidic gas, X , by finding the mass of a measured volume of gas X . subject: A-Level Chemistry
question_text: In this experiment you will identify the metal, M , in a metal carbonate, M CO 3 , by thermal decomposition. M CO FA 4 is the metal carbonate, M CO 3 . (a) Method ├бРђћ┬Ј Weigh the empty crucible with its lid. Record the mass. ├бРђћ┬Ј Transfer all the FA 4 from the container into the crucible. ├бРђћ┬Ј Weigh the crucible, lid and FA 4 . Record the mass. ├бРђћ┬Ј Calculate and record the mass of FA 4 used. ├бРђћ┬Ј Place the crucible and contents on a pipe-clay triangle. ├бРђћ┬Ј Heat the crucible gently, with the lid on, for approximately 1 minute. ├бРђћ┬Ј Heat strongly, with the lid off, for a further 4 minutes. ├бРђћ┬Ј Replace the lid and leave the crucible to cool for at least 5 minutes. During the cooling period, you may wish to begin work on Question 3. ├бРђћ┬Ј When the crucible has cooled, weigh the crucible with its lid and contents. Record the mass. ├бРђћ┬Ј Heat strongly, with the lid off, for a further 2 minutes. ├бРђћ┬Ј Replace the lid and leave the crucible to cool for at least 5 minutes. ├бРђћ┬Ј When the crucible has cooled, reweigh the crucible with its lid and contents. Record the mass. ├бРђћ┬Ј Calculate and record the total loss of mass and the mass of residue obtained. ├бРђћ┬Ј This residue is FA 5 . Keep FA 5 for use in 2(d). Results [5] (b) Calculations (i)   Calculate  the  amount,  in  mol,  of  carbon  dioxide  given  off  in  your  experiment. amount of CO 2 = mol [1] I II III IV V 5 9701/33/M/J/22 [Turn over (ii) Calculate the relative formula mass, M r , of M CO 3 . M r of M CO 3 = [1] (iii) From your results, deduce the identity of M . Show your reasoning. M is = [1] (c) A student carries out the same procedure, using the same mass of solid. However, the student uses the basic carbonate, M CO 3 ├бРѓг┬б M (OH) 2 , instead of the pure carbonate, M CO 3 . When the metal hydroxide part of the basic carbonate decomposes, metal oxide and steam are produced. The metal carbonate part decomposes in the usual way. State how the loss of mass from the student├бРѓгРёбs solid compares with the loss of mass you obtained when you carried out your experiment. Explain your reasoning. [2] (d) Use a spatula to transfer a small quantity of your cold residue, FA 5 , into a test-tube. Add about a 1 cm depth of dilute hydrochloric acid to the FA 5 in the test-tube. Record what you observe. State whether or not the thermal decomposition of M CO 3 is complete. Justify your answer based on your observations. question_text: Molecule M is present in petrol, a fuel used in cars. M is a saturated, non-cyclic hydrocarbon. M contains eight carbon atoms. question_text: O 3 2├бРѓгРђю , decomposes when an acid is added. S O 3 2├бРѓгРђю (aq) + 2H + (aq) S(s) + SO O(l) The rate of this reaction can be investigated by measuring how long it takes the solution to produce enough sulfur so that it cannot be seen through. You will investigate how the concentration of the thiosulfate ion affects the rate of the reaction. Note: A small amount of sulfur dioxide gas may be formed in the experiment. It is very important that you avoid inhaling any fumes. As soon as each experiment is complete, empty the reaction mixture into the quenching bath and rinse the beaker thoroughly. FA 1 is 0.100 mol dm ├бРѓгРђю3 sodium thiosulfate, Na O 3 . FA 2 is 2.00 mol dm ├бРѓгРђю3 hydrochloric acid, HC l . (a) Method Experiment 1 ├бРѓг┬б Fill a burette with FA 1 . ├бРѓг┬б Run 40.00 cm 3 of FA 1 into the 100 cm 3 beaker. ├бРѓг┬б Use the 25 cm 3 measuring cylinder to measure 10.0 cm 3 of FA 2 . ├бРѓг┬б Add the FA 2 to the FA 1 in the beaker and start timing immediately . ├бРѓг┬б Stir the mixture once and place the beaker on the printed insert. ├бРѓг┬б View the printing on the insert from above, through the solution. ├бРѓг┬б Stop timing when the print on the insert becomes obscured. ├бРѓг┬б Record this reaction time to the nearest second in the space for results on page 4. ├бРѓг┬б Empty the contents of the beaker into the quenching bath. ├бРѓг┬б Rinse and dry the beaker and glass rod so they are ready to use in Experiment 2. 4 9701/31/M/J/23 Experiment 2 ├бРѓг┬б Refill the burette with FA 1 . ├бРѓг┬б Fill the other burette with distilled water. ├бРѓг┬б Run 20.00 cm 3 of FA 1 into the 100 cm 3 beaker. ├бРѓг┬б Run 20.00 cm 3 of distilled water into the same beaker. ├бРѓг┬б Use the 25 cm 3 measuring cylinder to measure 10.0 cm 3 of FA 2 . ├бРѓг┬б Add the FA 2 to the solution in the beaker and start timing immediately . ├бРѓг┬б Stir the mixture once and place the beaker on the printed insert. ├бРѓг┬б View the printing on the insert from above, through the solution. ├бРѓг┬б Stop timing when the print on the insert becomes obscured. ├бРѓг┬б Record this reaction time to the nearest second. ├бРѓг┬б Empty the contents of the beaker into the quenching bath. ├бРѓг┬б Rinse and dry the beaker and glass rod so they are ready to use in the next experiment. Experiments 3├бРѓгРђю5 ├бРѓг┬б Carry out three further experiments to investigate how using different volumes of FA 1 affects the reaction time. Note that the combined volumes of FA 1 and distilled water must always be 40.00 cm 3 . Do not use a volume of FA 1 that is less than 15.00 cm 3 . Record all your results in a table. You should include the volume of FA 1 , the volume of distilled water, the reaction time and the reaction rate for each of your five experiments. question_text: In this experiment you will determine the relative atomic mass, A r , of metal M by thermal decomposition of its basic carbonate, M CO 3 ├бРѓг┬б M (OH) 2 . FB 1 is the basic metal carbonate, M CO 3 ├бРѓг┬б M (OH) 2 . (a) Method ├бРѓг┬б Weigh the empty crucible with its lid. Record the mass. ├бРѓг┬б Transfer all of the FB 1 from the container into the crucible. ├бРѓг┬б Weigh the crucible, lid and FB 1 . Record the mass. ├бРѓг┬б Calculate and record the mass of FB 1 used. ├бРѓг┬б Place the crucible and contents on a pipe-clay triangle. ├бРѓг┬б Heat the crucible gently, with the lid on, for approximately 1 minute. ├бРѓг┬б Heat strongly, with the lid off, for a further 4 minutes. ├бРѓг┬б Replace the lid and leave the crucible to cool for at least 5 minutes. During the cooling period, you may wish to begin work on Question 3. ├бРѓг┬б When the crucible is cool, weigh the crucible with its lid and contents. Record the mass. ├бРѓг┬б Place the crucible and contents on the pipe-clay triangle. Remove the lid. ├бРѓг┬б Heat the crucible strongly for a further 2 minutes. ├бРѓг┬б Replace the lid and leave the crucible to cool for at least 5 minutes. ├бРѓг┬б When the crucible is cool, reweigh the crucible with its lid and contents. Record the mass. ├бРѓг┬б Calculate and record the mass of residue obtained. Results [5] (b) Calculations (i) When FB 1 undergoes thermal decomposition, the products are the metal oxide, M O, carbon dioxide and water vapour. Give the equation for the thermal decomposition of FB 1 . Include state symbols. [1] I II III IV V 3 9701/32/M/J/23 [Turn over (ii) The amount, in mol, of carbon dioxide produced is given by the following formula. amount of CO 2 = mass loss during heating ( M r of CO 2 + M r of water) Calculate the amount, in mol, of carbon dioxide produced in (a) . amount of CO 2 = mol [1] (iii) Calculate the relative formula mass, M r , of the basic metal carbonate. Show your working. M r of M CO 3 ├бРѓг┬б M (OH) 2 = [1] (iv) Calculate the relative atomic mass of metal M . A r of M = [1] (c) A student accidentally spilt a little of the residue before carrying out the final weighing. Predict whether the calculated value of the relative atomic mass of M will be higher or lower as a result of this mistake. Explain your answer. The A r of M will be . explanation [1] (d) A student suggested that addition of sulfuric acid to the residue from (a) would show whether the basic metal carbonate had decomposed fully. State whether the student is correct. Explain your answer. question_text: OH, and the iron( III ) ion, Fe 3+ , in acidic conditions. The iron( III ) ion is reduced to an iron( II ) ion, Fe 2+ . question_text: FA 1 is impure calcium carbonate, CaCO 3 . FA 2 is 0.500 mol dm ├бРѓгРђю3 hydrochloric acid, HCl . (a) Method ├бРѓг┬б Weigh the container with FA 1. Record the mass in the space for results. ├бРѓг┬б Fill the tub with water to a depth of approximately 5 cm. ├бРѓг┬б Fill the 250 cm 3 measuring cylinder completely with water. Holding a piece of paper towel firmly over the top, invert the measuring cylinder and place it in the water in the tub. ├бРѓг┬б Remove the paper towel and clamp the inverted measuring cylinder so the open end is in the water just above the base of the tub. ├бРѓг┬б Using the 50 cm 3 measuring cylinder, transfer 50.0 cm 3 of FA 2 into the flask labelled X. Check that the bung fits tightly into the neck of flask X, clamp flask X and place the end of the delivery tube into the inverted 250 cm 3 measuring cylinder. ├бРѓг┬б Remove the bung from the neck of the flask. Tip all of the FA 1 into the acid in the flask and replace the bung immediately. Remove the flask from the clamp and swirl it to mix the contents. ├бРѓг┬б Replace the flask in the clamp and leave until the fizzing stops. Swirl the flask occasionally. ├бРѓг┬б Weigh the container with any residual FA 1. Record the mass. ├бРѓг┬б Calculate and record the mass of FA 1 that is added to the acid. ├бРѓг┬б When no more gas is collected, record the final volume of gas. You may wish to start Question 2 or Question 3 while the gas is being collected. subject: A-Level Chemistry
question_text: Acids donate H + in aqueous solution. The number of moles of H + donated per mole of acid is the proticity of the acid. For example, sulfuric acid is diprotic as it donates two moles of H + per mole of acid. In this experiment you will carry out a titration to determine the proticity of citric acid, C Method ├бРѓг┬б Support one of the cups in the 250 cm 3 beaker. ├бРѓг┬б Use the 50 cm 3 measuring cylinder to transfer 30.0 cm 3 of distilled water into the cup. ├бРѓг┬б Measure the temperature of the water in the cup. Record this temperature in the space for results. ├бРѓг┬б Weigh the container with FB 4. Record the mass. ├бРѓг┬б Tip all of the FB 4 into the water in the cup. ├бРѓг┬б Stir the mixture until the minimum temperature is obtained.
